_source: PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board), USP standards, Pharmacy law, Pharmaceutical calculations
_note: Long-tail pharmacy technician knowledge. Medication classifications, dosage calculations, compounding, inventory, insurance, regulations.

dosage_calculations.basic_formula: Dose ordered / Dose on hand × Quantity = Amount to give; Example: Order 500mg, Have 250mg tablets, Give 2 tablets
dosage_calculations.weight_based: mg/kg × patient weight (kg) = total dose; Example: 10 mg/kg for 70 kg patient = 700 mg total dose; then apply basic formula
dosage_calculations.ratio_proportion: Have/Want = Have/Want; cross-multiply; Example: 250mg/tablet = 500mg/X tablets, X = 2 tablets
dosage_calculations.pediatric_dosing: Always weight-based; verify dose with reference; double-check math; weight in kg (lbs ÷ 2.2 = kg); BSA (body surface area) for some drugs

concentration_dilution.percentage_strength: % = grams per 100 mL (w/v) or 100g (w/w); 5% = 5g per 100mL; used for creams, solutions
concentration_dilution.dilution_formula: C1 × V1 = C2 × V2 (initial concentration × initial volume = final concentration × final volume); Example: dilute 10mL of 50% to 20%, C1(50) × V1(10) = C2(20) × V2, V2 = 25mL total (add 15mL diluent)

iv_flow_rate.drops_per_minute: Volume (mL) × Drop factor / Time (min) = gtts/min; Example: 1000mL over 8hrs, drop factor 10gtts/mL = 1000 × 10 / 480 = 21 gtts/min
iv_flow_rate.ml_per_hour: Volume / Hours = mL/hr; Example: 1000mL over 8hrs = 125 mL/hr; set on infusion pump
iv_flow_rate.mcg_per_min: Dose (mcg/min) × 60 min / Concentration (mcg/mL) = mL/hr; Example: dopamine 400mcg/min from 400mg/250mL bag = 400mcg/min × 60 / 1600mcg/mL = 15 mL/hr
iv_flow_rate.drop_factors: Macrodrip 10, 15, or 20 gtts/mL; Microdrip 60 gtts/mL; must know tubing set drop factor

alligation.purpose: Mixing two concentrations to get intermediate concentration; Example: mix 20% and 5% to get 10%
alligation.method: Tic-tac-toe grid; higher concentration top-left, lower bottom-left, desired center, subtract diagonally; ratio of parts; Example: 20% and 5% to make 10% = 5 parts 20% + 10 parts 5% (simplify to 1:2 ratio)
alligation.calculation: Determine total volume needed; apply ratio; Example: need 300mL of 10%, ratio 1:2, so 100mL of 20% + 200mL of 5%

reconstitution.powder_volume: When adding diluent, powder displaces volume; final volume > diluent volume; label states final concentration after reconstitution
reconstitution.example: Add 10mL sterile water to 1g powder, final volume 10.5mL, final concentration 1g/10.5mL = 95mg/mL; follow package insert
reconstitution.beyond_use_date: Reconstituted drugs have short stability; label BUD per insert; refrigerate if required; typically 7-14 days refrigerated

antihypertensives.ace_inhibitors: Lisinopril, Enalapril, Ramipril; block ACE enzyme; lower BP; -pril suffix; SE: dry cough, hyperkalemia, angioedema; avoid in pregnancy
antihypertensives.arbs: Losartan, Valsartan, Irbesartan; block angiotensin II receptor; lower BP; -sartan suffix; SE: hyperkalemia, dizziness; alternative to ACE if cough
antihypertensives.beta_blockers: Metoprolol, Atenolol, Carvedilol; block beta receptors; lower HR and BP; -lol suffix; SE: bradycardia, fatigue, mask hypoglycemia; taper when discontinuing
antihypertensives.calcium_channel_blockers: Amlodipine, Diltiazem, Nifedipine; block calcium channels; vasodilation; -dipine suffix (dihydropyridines); SE: peripheral edema, flushing, constipation (diltiazem)
antihypertensives.diuretics: Furosemide (Lasix, loop), HCTZ (thiazide), Spironolactone (K-sparing); remove fluid; lower BP; SE: dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, hyperglycemia

antiarrhythmics.class_i: Sodium channel blockers; quinidine, procainamide, lidocaine; rarely used now
antiarrhythmics.class_iii: Potassium channel blockers; amiodarone (multiple SE: thyroid, lung, liver), sotalol, dofetilide (requires hospitalization for initiation)
antiarrhythmics.other: Digoxin (increases contractility, slows HR; narrow therapeutic index, toxicity risk), Adenosine (PSVT, brief asystole)

anticoagulants_antiplatelets.warfarin: Coumadin; Vitamin K antagonist; INR monitoring (target 2-3 typically); many drug/food interactions (Vitamin K foods); antidote: Vitamin K
anticoagulants_antiplatelets.doacs: Direct oral anticoagulants; rivaroxaban (Xarelto), apixaban (Eliquis), dabigatran (Pradaxa); no routine monitoring; less interactions; expensive; reversal agents available but limited
anticoagulants_antiplatelets.heparin_lmwh: Injectable; heparin (IV, monitor aPTT), enoxaparin/Lovenox (subQ, no monitoring); antidote: protamine
anticoagulants_antiplatelets.antiplatelets: Aspirin 81mg (irreversible platelet inhibition), Clopidogrel/Plavix (P2Y12 inhibitor, prodrug, requires activation), Ticagrelor/Brilinta (P2Y12, reversible)

cholesterol.statins: Atorvastatin (Lipitor), Simvastatin, Rosuvastatin (Crestor); HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors; lower LDL; -statin suffix; SE: muscle pain (myopathy, check CK), liver enzymes (monitor); take at night (cholesterol synthesis peaks overnight)
cholesterol.ezetimibe: Zetia; inhibits cholesterol absorption; lower LDL; add-on to statin; minimal SE
cholesterol.pcsk9_inhibitors: Evolocumab (Repatha), Alirocumab (Praluent); injectable; very effective LDL lowering; expensive; resistant hyperlipidemia

insulin.rapid_acting: Lispro (Humalog), Aspart (Novolog), Glulisine (Apidra); onset 15 min, peak 1hr, duration 4hr; mealtime dosing
insulin.short_acting: Regular (Humulin R, Novolin R); onset 30min, peak 2-3hr, duration 6-8hr; mealtime or IV
insulin.intermediate: NPH (Humulin N, Novolin N); onset 2-4hr, peak 4-10hr, duration 12-18hr; basal; cloudy suspension; mix before use
insulin.long_acting: Glargine (Lantus), Detemir (Levemir); onset 2-4hr, no peak, duration 24hr; basal once or twice daily; clear; cannot mix
insulin.ultra_long: Degludec (Tresiba); duration 42hr; ultra-stable basal; flexible dosing time
insulin.mixing: Can mix rapid/short with NPH (draw clear before cloudy); cannot mix with long-acting; premixed combos available (70/30, 75/25)
insulin.storage: Unopened: refrigerate; opened: room temp 28 days (pens), 42 days (vials); do not freeze; discard if discolored or clumpy (except NPH cloudy)

oral_agents.metformin: First-line Type 2; decreases hepatic glucose production; SE: GI upset (take with food, start low), lactic acidosis (rare, hold if contrast dye or surgery); contraindicated in renal impairment (eGFR <30)
oral_agents.sulfonylureas: Glipizide, Glyburide, Glimepiride; stimulate insulin release; -ide suffix; SE: hypoglycemia, weight gain; take before meals
oral_agents.sglt2_inhibitors: Empagliflozin (Jardiance), Canagliflozin (Invokana), Dapagliflozin (Farxiga); block glucose reabsorption in kidney; glucose in urine; -gliflozin suffix; SE: UTI, yeast infections, euglycemic DKA (rare), dehydration; cardiovascular and renal benefits
oral_agents.glp1_agonists: Semaglutide (Ozempic, Wegovy), Dulaglutide (Trulicity), Liraglutide (Victoza); incretin mimetic; injectable; -glutide suffix; SE: nausea, weight loss (desired), pancreatitis (rare), thyroid tumors (animal studies); cardiovascular benefits
oral_agents.dpp4_inhibitors: Sitagliptin (Januvia), Linagliptin (Tradjenta), Saxagliptin (Onglyza); increase incretin levels; -gliptin suffix; SE: minimal, weight neutral; less effective than GLP-1

antibiotics.penicillins: Amoxicillin, Ampicillin, Penicillin VK; beta-lactam; cell wall synthesis inhibition; -cillin suffix; SE: allergic reaction (rash to anaphylaxis); augmentin = amoxicillin + clavulanate (beta-lactamase inhibitor)
antibiotics.cephalosporins: Cephalexin (Keflex, 1st gen), Cefuroxime (2nd), Ceftriaxone (3rd); beta-lactam; ceph- or cef- prefix; generations 1-5; cross-allergy with penicillin ~10%; SE: similar to penicillins
antibiotics.macrolides: Azithromycin (Z-pack), Clarithromycin, Erythromycin; protein synthesis inhibition; -mycin suffix; SE: GI upset, QT prolongation (azithro); penicillin alternative
antibiotics.fluoroquinolones: Ciprofloxacin, Levofloxacin, Moxifloxacin; DNA gyrase inhibition; -floxacin suffix; broad spectrum; SE: tendon rupture (black box), QT prolongation, C.diff risk; avoid in children (cartilage damage)
antibiotics.tetracyclines: Doxycycline, Minocycline; protein synthesis inhibition; SE: photosensitivity, teeth staining (children <8), esophagitis (take with water, sit upright); avoid dairy (chelation reduces absorption)
antibiotics.others: Sulfamethoxazole/Trimethoprim (Bactrim, Septra; UTI, MRSA, PCP prophylaxis; SE: sulfa allergy, hyperkalemia), Metronidazole (Flagyl; anaerobes, C.diff; SE: metallic taste, disulfiram reaction with alcohol), Vancomycin (MRSA, C.diff oral; nephrotoxic, ototoxic; monitor levels)

opioids.schedule_ii: Oxycodone, Hydrocodone, Morphine, Fentanyl, Methadone; high abuse potential; no refills; written prescription only (some states allow e-script); pharmacist must verify legitimacy
opioids.schedule_iii: Codeine combinations <90mg; limited refills; moderate abuse potential
opioids.schedule_iv: Tramadol; lower abuse potential; up to 5 refills in 6 months
opioids.conversion: Morphine equivalents; oxycodone 1.5× potent as morphine; hydrocodone 1×; fentanyl patch 100× (50mcg/hr patch = 5mg/hr morphine = 120mg oral morphine/day)
opioids.naloxone: Narcan; opioid reversal; intranasal 4mg or injectable; OTC in many states; dispense with high-dose opioids

benzodiazepines.short_acting: Alprazolam (Xanax), Lorazepam (Ativan), Triazolam (Halcion); -azepam or -azolam suffix; Schedule IV; anxiety, insomnia
benzodiazepines.long_acting: Diazepam (Valium), Clonazepam (Klonopin); longer half-life; seizures, muscle spasm, anxiety
benzodiazepines.risks: Dependence, tolerance, withdrawal (seizures if abrupt stop); respiratory depression (especially with opioids, alcohol); taper when discontinuing

stimulants.schedule_ii: Amphetamine salts (Adderall), Methylphenidate (Ritalin, Concerta), Lisdexamfetamine (Vyvanse); ADHD; high abuse potential; no refills
stimulants.monitoring: Growth suppression in children; cardiovascular effects (BP, HR); appetite suppression; insomnia (dose early in day)

non_sterile.equipment: Mortar and pestle (grinding), spatula (mixing), ointment slab (glass), graduate cylinders (measuring), balance (weighing Class III prescription balance, sensitivity 6mg)

techniques.trituration: Grinding solid to fine powder; mortar and pestle; reduces particle size
techniques.levigation: Grinding with small amount liquid (levigating agent); makes smooth paste; for ointments
techniques.spatulation: Mixing with spatula on ointment slab; for combining ointments, creams
techniques.geometric_dilution: Mix small amount potent drug with equal amount diluent/base; double each time; ensures uniform distribution; prevents hot spots

formulations.solutions: Drug dissolved in solvent (water, alcohol); clear, homogeneous
formulations.suspensions: Drug particles dispersed in liquid; shake well; settles on standing; example: amoxicillin suspension
formulations.ointments: 80% oil, 20% water; greasy; petrolatum base; occlusive
formulations.creams: 50/50 oil/water; less greasy than ointment; absorbs better; vanishing cream base
formulations.capsules: Fill gelatin capsules; punch method (powder in pile, scoop) or capsule machine
formulations.suppositories: Rectal or vaginal; cocoa butter or PEG base; melt, add drug, pour into mold, cool

non_sterile.beyond_use_dating: USP <795>; non-aqueous formulations 6 months; water-containing oral 14 days; topical/dermal/mucosal 30 days; all at room temp or refrigerated per formulation; cannot exceed shortest expiration of ingredients

sterile_compounding.usp_797: Standards for sterile compounding; risk levels (low, medium, high); cleanroom requirements; environmental monitoring; personnel training/testing
sterile_compounding.cleanroom: ISO Class 7 (10,000 particles >0.5 micron per cubic ft); buffer room; anteroom (ISO 8); positive pressure; HEPA filtration
sterile_compounding.laminar_flow_hood: ISO Class 5 (100 particles); horizontal or vertical flow; HEPA filter; primary engineering control; 30-min warm-up; clean before use (70% IPA, wipe toward HEPA)
sterile_compounding.aseptic_technique: Sterile gloves, gown, hair cover, face mask; hand hygiene; no touching critical sites (needle tips, vial tops, ampule necks); work in center of hood 6 inches from sides

risk_levels.low_risk: Simple transfers; single-dose vials; 12-hr RT, 24-hr refrigerated, 45-day frozen (with passing sterility test)
risk_levels.medium_risk: Multiple dose vials, multiple transfers, long preparation time; 30-hr RT, 9-day refrigerated, 45-day frozen
risk_levels.high_risk: Non-sterile ingredients made sterile (terminal sterilization); open systems; 24-hr RT, 3-day refrigerated, 45-day frozen

sterile_compounding.iv_admixture: Add drug to IV bag or syringe; swab vial tops (70% IPA, dry 10 sec); draw up drug (withdraw air first if vial); inject into IV port; invert bag gently (no shaking); label with drug, dose, date/time, initials, BUD

hazardous_drugs.usp_800: Hazardous drug handling; separate areas; closed-system transfer devices (CSTD); protective equipment (chemo gloves, gown, face shield); negative pressure room
hazardous_drugs.examples: Chemotherapy (antineoplastics), hormones, some antivirals, immunosuppressants; NIOSH list of hazardous drugs
hazardous_drugs.spill_kit: Gown, double gloves, face shield, absorbent pads, scoop/brush, waste bags; small spill <5mL (tech can clean with training); large spill (call supervisor, evacuate)

prescription_processing.receiving: Verify patient info, prescriber, drug, strength, quantity, directions, refills, DAW (dispense as written); scan/enter into system; insurance adjudication
prescription_processing.daw_codes: 0 = substitution allowed, generic if available; 1 = prescriber DAW (brand medically necessary); 2 = patient requests brand (pay difference); 9 = generic not in stock
prescription_processing.data_entry: Accurate entry; NDC number; double-check similar names (look-alike/sound-alike); quantity, days supply, directions (SIG codes); allergies, interactions
prescription_processing.fill: Retrieve drug (check NDC, lot, expiration); count or pour (automated or manual); recount to verify; label (patient name, drug, strength, quantity, directions, prescriber, date, RX#, pharmacy info, warnings)
prescription_processing.verification: Pharmacist verifies: right patient, drug, dose, quantity, directions; checks for interactions, appropriateness; counsels patient or offers counseling; signs off
prescription_processing.pickup: Verify patient identity (name, DOB, address); counsel (new RX, changes, questions); provide written info (MedGuide, CMI); document counseling

insurance.adjudication: Submit claim electronically; response: approved, rejected, or prior authorization required; real-time processing

reject_codes.70_product_not_covered: Not on formulary; try alternative or prior authorization
reject_codes.75_prior_authorization: PA required; prescriber must justify medical necessity; submit to insurance
reject_codes.79_refill_too_soon: Days supply not elapsed; override if vacation, lost medication (with approval), or early refill allowed
reject_codes.65_patient_not_covered: Insurance inactive, terminated, or wrong info; verify eligibility, update info

insurance.copay_vs_coinsurance: Copay = fixed amount ($10, $30); Coinsurance = percentage (20%); deductible must be met first (patient pays full cost until deductible reached)
insurance.medicare_parts: Part A (hospital), Part B (medical/outpatient), Part C (Medicare Advantage, private insurance), Part D (prescription drugs); donut hole (coverage gap, pays more in gap until catastrophic coverage)

inventory_management.ordering: Automated (perpetual inventory); par levels (reorder point); minimize inventory (cost, storage, expiration); order frequently (weekly) in small quantities
inventory_management.receiving: Check against invoice; verify NDC, quantity, lot, expiration; date received; put away promptly; rotate stock (FEFO - first expire, first out)
inventory_management.returns: Expired drugs (return to wholesaler if <1 year expired, else destroy); recalled drugs (follow FDA/manufacturer instructions); wrong drug ordered (return within timeframe)
inventory_management.controlled_substances: Perpetual inventory (track every tablet); biannual inventory (count all C-II, document); C-II separate locked storage; DEA 222 form (old) or CSOS (electronic) for ordering; destroyed drugs require DEA 41 and witness
inventory_management.recalled_drugs: Check FDA recalls regularly; pull from shelves; quarantine; notify patients if dispensed; document
inventory_management.expiration_dating: Check monthly; rotate stock; 1-year rule (if drug expires <1 year, date prescription with expiration date); most prescriptions dated 1 year from fill or expiration, whichever sooner

medication_safety.look_alike_sound_alike: LASA drugs; HydrOXYzine vs hydrALAZINE; metFORMIN vs metroNIDAZOLE; tall man letters, separate storage, extra verification
medication_safety.high_alert_medications: Insulin, heparin, chemotherapy, narcotics, potassium; extra verification; independent double-check; potential for harm if error
medication_safety.error_prevention: Barcode scanning (verify 5 rights); independent double-check (high-alert drugs); do not disturb zone (reduce distractions); report near-misses; analyze root cause
medication_safety.five_rights: Right patient, drug, dose, route, time; verify each; prevent medication errors

prescriptions.required_elements: Patient name/DOB/address, prescriber name/DEA#/signature, date, drug/strength/dosage form, quantity, directions, refills; missing elements = invalid
prescriptions.verbal_orders: Phone or verbal from prescriber; allowed for non-controlled (most states); C-III to C-V allowed verbal in emergency (limited quantity, written follow-up within 7 days); C-II no verbal except emergency supply (72-hr, written within 7 days)
prescriptions.electronic_prescriptions: EPCS (Electronic Prescribing for Controlled Substances); allowed for all schedules if prescriber and pharmacy certified; more secure than written; required in some states
prescriptions.refill_limits: C-II no refills; C-III and C-IV up to 5 refills in 6 months; C-V varies by state; non-controlled typically 1 year from date or 12 refills
prescriptions.transfer_prescriptions: Non-controlled can transfer unlimited (original destroyed or voided); C-III to C-V one-time transfer (original voided); C-II no transfer; electronic refill transfer allowed (not one-time limit)

dea_schedules.schedule_i: No accepted medical use; high abuse potential; heroin, LSD, marijuana (federally, states vary); research only
dea_schedules.schedule_ii: High abuse potential; accepted medical use; severe dependence; morphine, oxycodone, amphetamine, cocaine; strict controls
dea_schedules.schedule_iii: Moderate abuse potential; moderate/low dependence; codeine <90mg, testosterone, ketamine, buprenorphine
dea_schedules.schedule_iv: Lower abuse potential; limited dependence; alprazolam, diazepam, tramadol, zolpidem
dea_schedules.schedule_v: Lowest abuse potential; cough preps with codeine <200mg/100mL; some states allow without prescription (must be 18+, log book)

hipaa.phi: Protected Health Information; name, DOB, address, phone, SSN, medical record number, diagnoses, medications; must protect
hipaa.minimum_necessary: Only access/disclose PHI necessary for job function; don't snoop; don't discuss patients outside work
hipaa.permitted_disclosures: Treatment, payment, operations (TPO); patient authorization; public health reporting; law enforcement with warrant
hipaa.violations: Civil penalties $100-50,000 per violation; criminal penalties up to $250,000 and 10 years prison for malicious; termination likely

state_boards.licensure: Technician registration or licensure (state-dependent); some require certification (PTCB or ExCPT); renewal every 1-3 years; CEU requirements
state_boards.scope_of_practice: Under pharmacist supervision; cannot counsel or interpret prescriptions; varies by state; know your state law
state_boards.discipline: Board can suspend, revoke, or place on probation for violations; drug diversion = automatic revocation and criminal charges; practice within scope

certification_exam.ptcb_exam: Pharmacy Technician Certification Exam; 90 questions, 2 hours; pass 1400/1600 score; 4 domains: Medications (40%), Federal Requirements (12.5%), Patient Safety/Quality Assurance (26.25%), Order Entry/Processing (21.25%)
certification_exam.eligibility: High school diploma or equivalent; comply with standards; no felony drug convictions; some states require training program
certification_exam.recertification: Every 2 years; 20 CE hours (1 hour pharmacy law, 1 hour patient safety); maintain certification to keep CPhT credential
certification_exam.study_tips: Brand/generic names (top 200 drugs); calculations (practice daily); law (DEA schedules, refills, transfers, HIPAA); compounding (USP chapters); patient safety
